stakeholders.primary_user: Senior product-designer candidate transitioning from a bank software department into a senior product-design role; intelligent, articulate, action-biased; lacks formal AI-product vocabulary and exposure
stakeholders.owner: Operator (gifts the app to the user); retains source
stakeholders.deployer: Operator deploys to personal GitHub + AWS commercial

requirements.statement: The system shall present a curriculum of eleven modules covering: model selection, sampling, harness development, context-file authoring, skills, chaining skills into workflows, automating workflows into routines, consolidating results, extracting results, summarizing results, and leadership guidance to a product team.
requirements.acceptance: Navigation lists exactly eleven modules with the named titles.

requirements.statement: Every curriculum module shall anchor concepts in a banking or fintech product scenario.
requirements.acceptance: Each module page renders at least one banking-scenario block (data fixture field `banking_scenario` non-empty).

requirements.statement: Every module shall include interview-ready vocabulary the user can quote verbatim.
requirements.acceptance: Each module renders a `vocabulary` list with >= 4 term/definition pairs.

requirements.statement: Every module shall include at least one interactive exercise that the user can complete in-app.
requirements.acceptance: Each module's `exercises` array has length >= 1; exercise renders an input control and a Submit affordance.

requirements.statement: When the user has provided an Anthropic API key, exercises shall be solvable via a live Claude call directly from the browser to api.anthropic.com.
requirements.acceptance: With a valid key configured, clicking Run on an exercise issues a POST to https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages with header `anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access: true` and renders the response.

requirements.statement: The system shall never transmit the user's API key to any host other than api.anthropic.com.
requirements.acceptance: Key is read only by the Anthropic client module; no analytics, telemetry, or third-party requests carry the key.

requirements.statement: The user's API key shall be stored only in the browser (localStorage) and shall never leave the device except as an Authorization header to api.anthropic.com.
requirements.acceptance: Source grep shows key referenced only in `lib/anthropic.ts` request builder; no server endpoint exists.

requirements.statement: The user shall be able to delete their API key from the app in one click.
requirements.acceptance: Settings exposes a Forget Key action that clears the key and reloads the settings panel state.

requirements.statement: The system shall track per-module progress (not-started, in-progress, completed) and persist it across reloads.
requirements.acceptance: Progress writes to localStorage and is restored on next mount; resetting clears all module state.

requirements.statement: The system shall be installable as a Progressive Web App on Android and iOS.
requirements.acceptance: manifest.webmanifest is served with name, icons (192/512/maskable), display=standalone, start_url; service worker is registered with autoUpdate.

requirements.statement: The system shall function offline for all non-exercise content after first load.
requirements.acceptance: After first load, navigating modules with the network disabled renders content from cache; only live-Claude exercises require network.

requirements.statement: The user shall be able to export a personalized 'leadership guidance brief' that consolidates their work into one document suitable for a product-team readout.
requirements.acceptance: Leadership view renders a structured brief and offers a download as Markdown (.md).

requirements.statement: The system shall offer a fully offline practice mode using canned example outputs so the user can complete every module without an API key.
requirements.acceptance: Each exercise has a `simulated` fallback that runs when no key is present.

requirements.statement: The UI shall avoid emoji iconography and use SVG icons (lucide) throughout.
requirements.acceptance: Source grep shows no emoji in components; all icon usages are lucide-react.

requirements.statement: The UI shall present a polished, banking-confident aesthetic (deep navy, paper warm-white, gold accent; serif display + sans body) and shall not read as terminal/monospace.
requirements.acceptance: Visual review against reference (Stripe/Mercury/Brex/Linear), no terminal aesthetic, mobile-first responsive.

requirements.statement: The user shall be able to choose between Claude models (Opus 4.7, Sonnet 4.6, Haiku 4.5) and adjust sampling parameters (temperature, max_tokens) for any live exercise.
requirements.acceptance: Settings exposes model select with the three model IDs and temperature/max_tokens controls; chosen values are sent in the request body.

requirements.statement: The system shall ship with a self-contained README explaining the curriculum, the BYO-key model, install steps, and an FAQ aimed at the end user.
requirements.acceptance: README.md present in repo root with the stated sections.

requirements.statement: The system shall be deployed to a public URL on GitHub Pages under the operator's personal account and to a public URL on AWS commercial S3 + (optional) CloudFront, with both URLs documented for the user.
requirements.acceptance: Both URLs return 200 and serve the built PWA; URLs are listed in README and on a deploy receipt.
